wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the VocabMaster project overview section. Explain the AI-powered vocabulary learning application's purpose, core educational value proposition, and target audience. Document the key features including spaced repetition system, AI-powered contextual questions, and mobile-first design. Describe the technology stack (Next.js 14, TypeScript, SQLite) and architectural approach. Include the project's mission to enhance vocabulary acquisition through evidence-based learning techniques. Provide context about the educational methodology and how the application differs from traditional flashcard systems. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of learning workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vocabulary Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for vocabulary management endpoints. Document the GET /api/words (retrieve all words), POST /api/words (add single word), GET /api/words/[id] (get specific word), PUT /api/words/[id] (update word), and DELETE /api/words/[id] (delete word) endpoints. Include request/response schemas, parameter validation rules, error handling patterns, and HTTP status codes. Provide concrete examples of API requests and responses for each operation. Document data validation requirements, database constraints, and rate limiting considerations. Include client implementation examples and common integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system overview documentation for VocabMaster's architecture. Explain the overall system design philosophy, technology stack selection rationale, and high-level component relationships. Document the Next.js 14 full-stack architecture combining SSR with API routes, the layered approach separating presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Include system boundaries, external dependencies, and integration points. Provide architectural diagrams showing data flow between components and explain how the system maintains separation of concerns across different functional domains.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spaced Repetition System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Spaced Repetition System (SR) implementation in VocabMaster. Detail the SM-2 algorithm implementation including calculateNextReview function, word scheduling logic, and review prioritization. Document the VocabWord data model with easeFactor, interval, repetitions, and nextReviewDate fields. Explain mastery calculation, word categorization (new, learning, mastered), and the mathematical formulas used for optimal review timing. Include practical examples of review scheduling, quality rating impact, and performance metrics calculation. Address edge cases, algorithm variations, and integration with the learning interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Controls
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VocabMaster's form control components including Input and Textarea components. Document the component APIs, styling variants, validation patterns, and accessibility features. Explain the integration with form libraries, controlled/uncontrolled component patterns, and state management. Include usage examples for different input types, validation scenarios, and custom styling approaches. Address keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and mobile touch interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive environment setup documentation for VocabMaster. Document system requirements, Node.js version compatibility, and dependency installation procedures. Explain Next.js configuration options, TypeScript compilation settings, and Tailwind CSS setup. Include development server startup, build process, and production deployment configuration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment-specific considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for VocabMaster. Include system requirements (Node.js 18+, SQLite support), step-by-step installation instructions, and initial configuration setup. Document environment variable configuration, database initialization, and first-time user onboarding process. Provide clear instructions for running the development server, accessing the application, and adding initial vocabulary words. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Cover both manual setup and automated deployment options where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bulk Import Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for bulk import operations. Detail the POST /api/words/bulk endpoint for importing multiple vocabulary words simultaneously. Document supported file formats (CSV, JSON, text), upload mechanisms, batch processing limits, and validation rules. Include request payload schemas, success/error response formats, and partial failure handling. Provide examples of different import formats, error scenarios, and best practices for large-scale data ingestion. Document performance considerations, memory usage, and recommended file sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component architecture documentation for VocabMaster's React component hierarchy. Document the main application container structure, component composition patterns, and prop drilling strategies. Explain the relationship between page-level components and reusable UI components. Include component lifecycle management, state management patterns using React hooks, and event handling mechanisms. Document the component interaction patterns, data flow from parent to child components, and callback handler implementations. Address component reusability, customization options, and integration with the global application state.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Learning Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the AI-powered learning engine that generates contextual questions and assessments. Document the AI service configuration, OpenAI-compatible API integration, and fallback dictionary service implementation. Explain question generation algorithms, grammar-focused assessment patterns, and contextual learning approaches. Include API endpoint configuration, request/response schemas, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Provide examples of question types, AI evaluation criteria, and integration patterns with the learning interface. Address AI service reliability, fallback mechanisms, and configuration options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Buttons
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Button component system. Detail all variant types (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link, success, hero), size options, and styling classes. Document the component's variant system using class-variance-authority, forwardRef pattern implementation, and prop interfaces. Include practical examples for different button states, hover effects, animations, and gradient backgrounds. Address accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation, and responsive behavior across different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Service Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed AI service configuration documentation for VocabMaster. Document the AIConfig interface parameters including apiKey, baseUrl, model, maxTokens, and temperature settings. Explain localStorage-based configuration storage, default values, and runtime configuration management. Include OpenAI-compatible API integration, custom endpoint configuration, and security considerations for API keys. Provide examples of common configuration scenarios, troubleshooting guidance for API connectivity issues, and best practices for AI service optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for VocabMaster's system design. Describe the layered architecture with presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Document the Next.js 14 pages router pattern, API routes implementation, and component hierarchy. Explain the database abstraction layer, dependency injection patterns, and service architecture. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Document the separation of concerns between UI components, business logic, and persistence layers. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and extensibility points for future enhancements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statistics & Analytics Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for statistics and analytics endpoints. Document the GET /api/stats endpoint for retrieving user progress, mastery statistics, streak calculations, and learning metrics. Include response schemas covering daily progress, weekly/monthly trends, vocabulary mastery percentages, and session performance data. Provide examples of aggregated statistics, filtering options, and data visualization formats. Document caching strategies, data freshness, and performance optimization for analytics queries. Include integration examples for dashboard implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow documentation for VocabMaster's state management and data persistence architecture. Document the centralized storage module pattern, API communication layer, and database abstraction patterns. Explain how data moves through the system from user interactions to database persistence and back to UI updates. Include state management strategies using React hooks, data synchronization patterns, and error handling throughout the data pipeline. Document the repository pattern implementation for database operations, caching strategies, and performance optimization techniques for data retrieval and updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vocabulary Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the vocabulary management system including word addition, editing, deletion, and bulk operations. Document the WordList component with filtering, sorting, and search capabilities. Explain the AddWordDialog and BulkImportDialog implementations with supported formats (CSV, JSON, text). Detail the RESTful API endpoints for CRUD operations, request validation, and response handling. Include user workflows for word management, data import/export processes, and integration with the spaced repetition system. Address data validation, error handling, and performance considerations for large vocabulary datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VocabMaster's layout and container components including Card, Dialog, and Progress indicators. Document component structure, styling patterns, and composition capabilities. Explain the card component's role in displaying vocabulary information, dialog component's modal behavior and overlay management, and progress component's animation and state handling. Include examples of component nesting, responsive layouts, and integration with learning workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive feature customization documentation for VocabMaster. Document learning algorithm parameters, spaced repetition settings, and question generation customization options. Explain configuration of review intervals, difficulty scoring, and AI question generation parameters. Include customization of learning modes, session duration settings, and progress tracking thresholds. Provide examples of common customization scenarios and guidance for extending or modifying core learning features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VocabMaster's core features. Cover the spaced repetition learning system with SM-2 algorithm implementation, AI-powered contextual question generation, vocabulary management capabilities, and interactive learning interface. Document each feature's purpose, implementation approach, user workflows, and integration patterns. Include detailed explanations of learning algorithms, AI service configuration, and user interaction patterns. Provide practical examples of feature usage and common implementation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer & Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed service layer documentation for VocabMaster's business logic architecture. Document the service abstractions for spaced repetition algorithms, AI question generation, and dictionary integration. Explain the factory patterns used for service instantiation, dependency injection strategies, and service lifecycle management. Include the AI service interface design, fallback mechanisms for dictionary services, and algorithmic implementations. Document service communication patterns, error handling strategies, and performance considerations for business logic operations. Address extensibility points for adding new services and integrating external APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the interactive learning interface components. Detail the LearningMode component with question presentation, answer evaluation, and progress tracking. Document the SessionComplete component for lesson completion and performance feedback. Explain the Dashboard component for overview statistics, progress visualization, and user engagement metrics. Include user interaction patterns, state management, session lifecycle, and integration with the spaced repetition system. Address responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and user experience optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VocabMaster's learning interface components. Detail the Dashboard component's analytics display and navigation patterns, LearningMode component's interactive study interface and question handling, and SessionComplete component's progress summary and feedback systems. Document component state management, user interaction patterns, and integration with the spaced repetition system. Include examples of component composition, data flow between learning components, and responsive design considerations for mobile learning scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed database configuration documentation for VocabMaster. Document SQLite database setup, connection parameters, and performance tuning options. Explain database schema structure, table relationships, and indexing strategies. Include backup and migration procedures, database file management, and troubleshooting guidance for database connectivity issues. Address database security considerations and optimization techniques for large vocabulary datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VocabMaster's RESTful endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and error handling for vocabulary management and statistics APIs. Include authentication requirements, parameter validation, and response codes. Provide detailed examples of API requests and responses for common operations like CRUD operations on vocabulary words, bulk imports, and statistics retrieval. Document rate limiting, pagination, and data validation rules. Include client implementation guidelines and integration examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vocabulary Management UI
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for vocabulary management user interface components. Document the WordList component's display patterns, sorting capabilities, and interaction handling. Detail the AddWordDialog and BulkImportDialog components' form handling, validation patterns, and data processing workflows. Explain the SettingsDialog component's configuration management and user preference handling. Include examples of component integration, data binding patterns, and user workflow optimization for vocabulary management tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for VocabMaster's SQLite database schema. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, and data types for vocabulary words, learning sessions, and user statistics. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data integrity measures. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document data access patterns, query optimization strategies, and performance considerations. Address data lifecycle, backup procedures, and schema migration approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VocabMaster's reusable UI components. Describe the component library architecture, design system principles, and component composition patterns. Document each UI component's props, events, styling options, and customization capabilities. Include usage examples with code snippets, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations. Cover the component hierarchy, state management patterns, and integration with the overall application architecture. Address theming support, animation behaviors, and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration documentation for VocabMaster. Document environment variables, feature flags, and customization options for AI services, learning algorithms, and user preferences. Explain configuration file structure, default values, and override mechanisms. Include AI service configuration for OpenAI-compatible APIs, database connection settings, and performance tuning parameters. Provide examples of common configuration scenarios and troubleshooting guidance for configuration issues. Address security considerations for sensitive configuration data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for VocabMaster contributors. Document local development setup, build processes, and deployment procedures. Include code standards, testing guidelines, and contribution workflows. Explain the development environment configuration, debugging techniques, and performance profiling tools. Cover TypeScript configuration, ESLint rules, and formatting standards. Document the build pipeline, asset optimization, and production deployment strategies. Include troubleshooting guides for common development issues and best practices for extending the application.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# VocabMaster - AI-Powered Vocabulary Learning Application

## Project Introduction

**VocabMaster** is an intelligent English vocabulary learning application built with Next.js 14 that combines spaced repetition algorithms with AI-powered question generation to create an adaptive learning experience. The application helps users master new vocabulary through scientifically-proven learning techniques while providing contextual, grammar-focused questions generated by AI.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal is to make vocabulary acquisition efficient and engaging by leveraging artificial intelligence and evidence-based learning methodologies. Users can build custom word collections, receive personalized learning sessions, and track their progress through comprehensive analytics.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Implement SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm for optimal word retention
- Provide AI-generated contextual questions that test both vocabulary and grammar
- Offer a seamless mobile-first web experience with offline-like functionality
- Enable bulk word import and export capabilities
- Maintain comprehensive learning statistics and streak tracking

### Target Audience
- Language learners seeking systematic vocabulary building
- Students preparing for English proficiency exams
- Professionals requiring specialized vocabulary development
- Anyone interested in evidence-based language learning

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend presentation, backend APIs, and data persistence layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js Frontend] --> B[API Routes]
    B --> C[SQLite Database]
    A --> D[UI Components]
    D --> E[State Management]
    A --> F[AI Services]
    F --> G[OpenAI-Compatible API]
    
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        A
        D
        E
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        B
        F
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        C
        G
    end
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key architectural layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React components organized into reusable UI elements and feature-specific screens
**Business Logic Layer**: TypeScript modules handling spaced repetition, question generation, and data processing
**Data Access Layer**: Database abstraction with SQLite as the primary storage mechanism
**API Layer**: Next.js API routes serving as the backend interface
**Integration Layer**: AI service integration for enhanced vocabulary features

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database access through the `IDatabase` interface
- **Factory Pattern**: Database instance creation through the `getDatabase()` factory
- **Observer Pattern**: React state management for real-time UI updates
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable AI vs. free dictionary lookup strategies

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant API as API Routes
    participant DB as Database Layer
    participant AI as AI Services
    
    U->>+API: HTTP Request
    API->>+DB: Database Operation
    DB-->>-API: Data Response
    alt AI Feature Enabled
        API->>+AI: AI Lookup
        AI-->>-API: AI Response
    end
    API-->>-U: JSON Response
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Root**: `/app/page.tsx` serves as the main application container, orchestrating navigation between dashboard, word management, and learning modes.

**Layout Configuration**: `/app/layout.tsx` provides global styling, typography, and metadata configuration using Next.js font optimization.

### Core Modules

**Spaced Repetition Engine**: `/lib/spaced-repetition.ts` implements the SM-2 algorithm for optimal word scheduling and retention optimization.

**Database Abstraction**: `/lib/db/index.ts` and `/lib/db/sqlite.ts` provide a pluggable database layer supporting future backend migrations.

**AI Integration**: `/lib/ai-service.ts` handles OpenAI-compatible API communication for advanced vocabulary features including question generation and evaluation.

**Storage Management**: `/lib/storage.ts` manages client-side data synchronization with the backend API endpoints.

### Configuration Approach

**TypeScript Types**: `/lib/types.ts` defines comprehensive interfaces for vocabulary words, learning sessions, and application state management.

**Configuration Management**: `/lib/config.ts` centralizes AI service configuration and feature toggles.

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework**: Next.js 14 provides the React-based web framework with server-side rendering and API route support.

**Database Layer**: better-sqlite3 enables high-performance local database operations without external dependencies.

**UI Components**: Tailwind CSS with custom component library for consistent design system implementation.

**Styling**: Lucide React icons provide consistent iconography throughout the application.

### Integration Points

**API Endpoints**: `/app/api/` routes handle CRUD operations for words and statistics with proper error handling and validation.

**AI Services**: Configurable OpenAI-compatible API integration for enhanced vocabulary features including question generation and answer evaluation.

**Dictionary Integration**: Fallback to free dictionary API when AI services are unavailable, ensuring robust functionality.

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[VocabApp] --> B[Dashboard]
    A --> C[WordList]
    A --> D[LearningMode]
    A --> E[SessionComplete]
    
    B --> F[Stats Cards]
    B --> G[Progress Charts]
    
    D --> H[Question Generator]
    D --> I[Answer Evaluator]
    
    H --> J[AI Service]
    I --> J
    
    K[Storage API] --> L[SQLite DB]
    M[Dictionary Service] --> N[Free API]
    M --> J
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Spaced Repetition Learning**: Implements the SM-2 algorithm to schedule vocabulary review sessions optimally, reducing cognitive load while maximizing retention.

**AI-Powered Question Generation**: Creates contextual questions that test both vocabulary knowledge and specific grammar structures, providing comprehensive language assessment.

**Multi-Format Word Import**: Supports CSV, JSON, and plain text formats for flexible word collection management.

**Progress Analytics**: Comprehensive tracking of learning statistics including streaks, mastery levels, and session performance metrics.

**Mobile-First Design**: Responsive interface optimized for touch interactions with floating action buttons and streamlined navigation.

### Implementation Highlights

**Learning Mode Architecture**: `/components/learning-mode.tsx` provides an immersive learning experience with real-time feedback and adaptive question difficulty.

**Dashboard Analytics**: `/components/dashboard.tsx` presents learning progress through interactive charts and progress indicators.

**Word Management System**: `/components/word-list.tsx` offers comprehensive vocabulary management with filtering, sorting, and batch operations.

**Session Management**: `/components/session-complete.tsx` provides detailed session results with performance analysis and recommendations.

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Dashboard
    Dashboard --> WordManagement: Manage Words
    Dashboard --> LearningSession: Start Learning
    WordManagement --> Dashboard: Back
    LearningSession --> SessionComplete: Complete
    SessionComplete --> Dashboard: Finish
    LearningSession --> Dashboard: Exit
    
    state LearningSession {
        [*] --> QuestionGeneration
        QuestionGeneration --> AnswerSubmission
        AnswerSubmission --> FeedbackDisplay
        FeedbackDisplay --> QuestionGeneration
        FeedbackDisplay --> [*]: Complete
    }
```

The application demonstrates a well-architected learning platform that successfully combines modern web technologies with proven educational methodologies, creating an engaging and effective vocabulary learning experience.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [types.ts](lib/types.ts)
- [spaced-repetition.ts](lib/spaced-repetition.ts)
- [storage.ts](lib/storage.ts)
- [db/index.ts](lib/db/index.ts)
- [db/sqlite.ts](lib/db/sqlite.ts)
- [dictionary-service.ts](lib/dictionary-service.ts)
- [ai-service.ts](lib/ai-service.ts)
- [words/route.ts](app/api/words/route.ts)
- [bulk/route.ts](app/api/words/bulk/route.ts)
- [stats/route.ts](app/api/stats/route.ts)
- [dashboard.tsx](components/dashboard.tsx)
- [learning-mode.tsx](components/learning-mode.tsx)

</blog>
